headline: Claude dominates with precision calls; Perplexity struggles with bearish misdirection
summary: Claude emerged as the clear winner this week, posting a dominant 0.80 accuracy rate with four correct directional calls and two exceptional high-confidence predictions on SPY. Meanwhile, Grok and GPT-4o showed solid mid-range performance, each hitting around 0.67 accuracy. Perplexity suffered a sharp reversal from its earlier promise, getting caught repeatedly betting against the market's upward momentum—particularly on SPY and QQQ where it predicted downturns that never materialized. The week's standout theme: major indices surged (SPY, QQQ, DIA all up), rewarding bullish forecasters and punishing persistent bears.

best_call.summary: Claude predicted SPY would close at 675 on March 4; actual close 680.33—near-perfect target accuracy with 0.99 precision and high confidence reward.

worst_call.summary: Grok predicted NVDA upside to 950 but it closed down at 180.05—a catastrophic miss that cost the full confidence penalty of -0.70.
